title: Twilight of World Capitalism
description: Twilight of World Capitalism is a non-fiction by William Z. Foster. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.
descriptionParagraphs: Twilight of World Capitalism is a non-fiction by William Z. Foster. Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Singapore. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned under the Internal Security (Prohibition of Publications) (Consolidation) Order. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: Twilight of World Capitalism entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Singapore, where Singapore authorities banned publication or circulation. counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
